message:
Fix TTY/shell activity detection, conformal predictor self-call, HSMM duration tracking, and LFP credal set allocation

- Improve TTY activity detection to check both access time and
  modification time, using the more recent of the two, since some
  kernels only update one timestamp depending on the I/O direction
- Fix shell activity detection to check the shell's stdin TTY device
  access time instead of the /proc stat file modification time, which
  reflects process metadata changes rather than actual user interaction
- Fix AdaptiveConformalRegressor::calibrate_with_feedback to call
  self.predict() instead of self.inner.predict(), ensuring the adaptive
  wrapper's adjusted alpha is used for coverage tracking
- Fix HSMM duration tracking off-by-one: report previous_duration as
  current_duration-1 (excluding the new state's first observation),
  update duration posterior with the corrected count, and reset
  current_duration to 1 instead of 0 since the transition observation
  belongs to the new state
- Rewrite LeastFavorablePrior probability allocation to start from each
  class's credal set lower bound (guaranteeing feasibility), then
  greedily allocate remaining probability to highest-loss classes up to
  their upper bounds, and compute final expected loss from the actual
  probability vector instead of accumulating during allocation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
